targetSlots.planningTest: Show what the protagonist wants, lacks, or cannot tolerate before the story changes.
Present the concrete path into the story problem.
End the opening with a choice or consequence that prevents easy retreat.
Reveal information or opposition that changes what the protagonist thinks the story is.
Confront the protagonist with the flaw, false belief, or missing knowledge.
Make victory depend on a meaningful choice, not only force or luck.

concept.premise: Rowan Ash, a royal forester who can hear the Ironwood crown roots, learns that the succession trial is poisoning the forest's future kings. To prevent a civil war, he must prove the chosen heir is feeding on a forbidden root before the forest crowns her.
concept.readerPromise: A forest-court fantasy adventure about inheritance, duty, and whether a kingdom should obey a living crown.
concept.centralConflict: Rowan needs the Ironwood's judgment respected, while Princess Elayne bends that judgment through poison and court law.

protagonist.name: Rowan Ash
protagonist.desire: serve as crown forester without entering noble politics
protagonist.fear: the forest choosing a ruler who will punish his family for speaking
protagonist.flaw: he treats neutrality as honor even when silence helps the corrupt

characters.name: Lena Moss
characters.materiality: A hedge healer who can identify root poison but belongs to a banned rural order.

characters.name: Prince Calen
characters.materiality: The losing heir may be honest or merely desperate enough to use Rowan.

characters.name: Princess Elayne
characters.materiality: The favored heir controls court witnesses and can make forest dissent look like treason.

worldFacts.fact: Ironwood crown roots bind to the next ruler and reveal lies by tightening around the speaker's shadow.
Forbidden ghostroot makes the Ironwood hear a false future but leaves silver veins in nearby leaves.
Court law says anyone who challenges a forest coronation must offer their own bloodline as collateral.

storyPromise.text: If Rowan proves the coronation is poisoned, he can stop the wrong heir but must choose whether neutrality is betrayal.

constraints: Produce planning contracts only, not prose.
Use scene contracts as the generation and adherence unit.
Make character and world obligations materially affect choices, costs, or consequences.
Keep the diagnostic to six chapter-contract units.
Preserve the Snowflake-lite strategy packet in chapter and scene contracts.
Route storyDebtId values into obligations and scene requiredSourceIds.

strategyPacket.logline: A neutral forester must prove a living crown is being poisoned.
strategyPacket.paragraphSummary: Rowan wants to serve the Ironwood without entering noble politics. Silver-veined leaves suggest the favored heir is feeding ghostroot to the crown roots. Lena can prove poison, but helping her makes Rowan complicit with a banned rural order. Princess Elayne turns court law against anyone challenging the forest. Rowan must risk his bloodline and reject neutrality to stop a poisoned coronation.
majorReversals: The Ironwood's chosen future may be a ghostroot hallucination.
Court law requires Rowan's bloodline as collateral for challenging the crown.
Neutral silence protects the poisoning more than the forest.
strategyPacket.endingDirection: Rowan offers his bloodline as collateral, exposes ghostroot, and rejects neutrality.
strategyPacket.readerPromise: A forest-court adventure where duty, succession, and living roots test whether silence is betrayal.
strategyPacket.protagonistWant: serve as crown forester without entering noble politics
strategyPacket.protagonistNeed: accept that neutrality becomes complicity when truth is poisoned
strategyPacket.protagonistLie: silence can remain honorable in a corrupt succession
strategyPacket.protagonistTruth: protecting the forest requires public risk
strategyPacket.antagonistPressure: Princess Elayne controls court witnesses and can brand forest dissent as treason.
strategyPacket.worldPressureRule: Crown roots reveal lies through shadows, while ghostroot makes them hear false futures.

storyDebts.promiseText: Rowan can stop the wrong heir if he proves the coronation is poisoned.
storyDebts.payoffPolicy: Pay off when Rowan proves ghostroot poisoning in public succession law.

storyDebts.promiseText: Rowan must decide whether neutrality is honor or betrayal.
storyDebts.payoffPolicy: Pay off when Rowan risks his own bloodline instead of staying silent.
